## Task Breakdown

### Sub-issue 214.1 â€” Scriber utility agent

#### T1: Design scriber agent intent
- **Sub-issue:** 214.1
- **Files:** Create `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/.meridian/project/issues/214/context/scriber-design.md`
- **Details:** Document the scriber's role: a utility agent invoked by plays to write evidence, checkpoint, and status files in the background while the orchestrator continues other work. Define its dispatch contract (JSON â€” what the caller passes, what the scriber returns). Define what counts as "evidence" vs what stays inline (plan output, user-visible artifacts stay inline; per-step traces, self-commit preparation, status updates go to scriber). Define failure semantics â€” what happens when scriber fails to write.
- **Depends on:** none
- **Expected Outcome:** A design note scoping the scriber's role before any code is written.
- **Verification:** Design note exists and is reviewable; clearly distinguishes blocking vs non-blocking writes.
- **Rollback plan:** Delete the file.

#### T2: Create `scriber` agent file
- **Sub-issue:** 214.1
- **Files:** Create `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/agents/scriber.md`
- **Details:** New utility agent following the pattern of `doc-builder.md`. Frontmatter: `name: scriber`, `domain: infra`, `role: evidence-writer`, `model: sonnet` (lightweight), `tools: [Read, Write, Skill]`. Identity: "autonomous background evidence-writer for plays". Capabilities table lists: `write-evidence`. Intent Recognition parses the play's JSON contract to determine target path, content, and timing. Boundaries: NEVER writes code or domain artifacts; only writes evidence / checkpoint / status files; never blocks the caller. Document in the agent file that scriber is a UTILITY agent and is exempt from the L2 â‰¤5 domain-agent budget per `feedback_agent_budget_scope.md`.
- **Depends on:** T1
- **Expected Outcome:** Agent file ready to be invoked by plays.
- **Verification:** File has frontmatter, Identity, Capabilities, Intent Recognition, Boundaries sections. `tools:` includes `Skill`.
- **Rollback plan:** Delete the file.

#### T3: Create `write-evidence` skill
- **Sub-issue:** 214.1
- **Files:** Create `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/skills/write-evidence/SKILL.md`
- **Details:** SKILL.md: input is a JSON payload containing target_path (whitelist-compliant), content (markdown or YAML), metadata (play_name, timestamp, issue_number). Process: ensure parent directories exist; write the file atomically (temp file + rename); on conflict, append timestamp suffix. Output: written file path + write confirmation. Constraints: NEVER write outside the whitelist; NEVER overwrite without explicit permission; fail loudly if parent directory is unreachable. Reference the whitelist explicitly â€” this skill is the gatekeeper for folder compliance at the write boundary.
- **Depends on:** T2
- **Expected Outcome:** Skill that the scriber agent invokes.
- **Verification:** SKILL.md has Purpose / Input / Process / Output / Constraints sections. Reference to whitelist is explicit.
- **Rollback plan:** Delete the skill directory.

#### T4: Adopt scriber in a reference play (`commit-code`)
- **Sub-issue:** 214.1
- **Files:**
  - Modify `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/plays/commit-code/reference/intent.yaml` â€” add a constraint stating that evidence writes are delegated to the scriber agent.
  - Modify (recompile) `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/plays/commit-code/SKILL.md` via `/create-play --build commit-code`.
- **Details:** commit-code currently writes evidence inline. Update its intent.yaml to declare evidence-writing is delegated to scriber. Recompile the play so the compiled SKILL.md dispatches the scriber agent instead of writing inline. Verify the existing scenario evals still pass â€” the change should be invisible to the user but non-blocking on evidence I/O.
- **Depends on:** T3
- **Expected Outcome:** commit-code uses scriber; existing evals still pass.
- **Verification:** Recompiled SKILL.md references scriber; scenario evals PASS; dry-run of commit-code against a fixture produces evidence files written by scriber.
- **Rollback plan:** git checkout on intent.yaml + SKILL.md.

#### T5: Document scriber in `docs/components/agents.md`
- **Sub-issue:** 214.1
- **Files:** Modify `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/docs/components/agents.md`
- **Details:** Add a scriber entry to the agents roster table with role, domain, skills, and intent-recognition notes. Add a short section explaining when plays should dispatch the scriber vs write inline. Reference the reference-adopter pattern from commit-code.
- **Depends on:** T4
- **Expected Outcome:** Docs match the new agent.
- **Verification:** Read agents.md; confirm the new entry and section.
- **Rollback plan:** git checkout.

### Sub-issue 214.2 â€” Folder whitelist & config reconciliation

- **Sub-issue:** 214.2
- **Files:** Create `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/docs/adr/017-folder-whitelist.md`
- **Details:** Full ADR covering (a) the strict `.meridian/` folder whitelist: `core/`, `product/{product,ux,arch}`, `project/issues/{n}/{specs,evidence,checkpoint,context,review}` â€” and NOTHING ELSE; (b) the explicit rejection of the Plan sub-agent's proposed amendment to add `evidence/checkpoints/status/` as siblings under `.meridian/product/`; (c) the pattern for routing ops artifacts INSIDE the whitelist buckets using underscore-prefixed subfolders (`.meridian/product/product/_checkpoints/spec-product/20260414.md`); (d) migration path from `product.directories` (discovery/roadmap/architecture/briefs) to `product/ux/arch`; (e) migration from `stm.structure` (spec/design/evidence/delivery/checkpoint) to `specs/evidence/checkpoint/context/review`; (f) rationale; (g) related ADRs (008 issue-centric STM, 014 three-axis profiling, 016 agent JSON contract). Status: Proposed.
- **Depends on:** none (depends on 214.1 completing)
- **Expected Outcome:** A reviewable ADR capturing the decision.
- **Verification:** File exists; structure matches other ADRs; amendment explicitly rejected.
- **Rollback plan:** Delete the file.

### Sub-issue 214.4 â€” Extend existing KB domain-taxonomy markdown + add cross-tree constraints

#### T18: Define extended markdown section conventions + intent-yaml-schema
- **Sub-issue:** 214.4
- **Files:**
  - Create `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/memory/standards/intent-yaml-schema.yaml` (or update if exists) â€” defines the canonical intent.yaml shape: metadata zone (`name`, `description`, `version`, `checksum`) + content zone (`intent`, `constraints`, `failure_conditions`, `scenarios`). Documents that `checksum` is `sha256` computed over the YAML-normalized content zone (excluding the `checksum` field itself; exact normalization spec: sort keys alphabetically at each nesting level, drop comments, use LF line endings, UTF-8 encoding, then hash the resulting byte stream). The `/create-play --build` step maintains the checksum; drift is detected when the computed checksum differs from the on-disk value.
  - Create `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/memory/standards/kb-extension-conventions.md` â€” defines the 5 new markdown sections every feature in `domain-taxonomy/*.md` must carry: `### Inclusion`, `### Success Criteria`, `### Failure Scenarios`, `### Cross-Tree Refs`, `### Experiential`. Documents section schemas (what each section must contain, how to parse them programmatically).
- **Details:** This task establishes the schema BEFORE extending markdown files. intent-yaml-schema captures the v2 shape with metadata and the sha256-over-normalized-content-zone checksum algorithm (confirmed at 214 approval gate 2026-04-14). kb-extension-conventions captures the five section headings and their structures. Linter task (T22) reads kb-extension-conventions to validate extended markdown files.
- **Depends on:** none (depends on 214.3 completing)
- **Expected Outcome:** Canonical schemas and conventions.
- **Verification:** Both files exist; intent-yaml-schema validates a sample intent.yaml with metadata + 4 content fields; the checksum algorithm is documented with the normalization spec.
- **Rollback plan:** Delete.

#### T20: Extend `user-management.md` and `commerce.md` with new sections
- **Sub-issue:** 214.4
- **Files:**
  - Modify `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/memory/knowledge/domain-taxonomy/user-management.md`
  - Modify `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/memory/knowledge/domain-taxonomy/commerce.md`
- **Details:** For EVERY feature (UM-F001, UM-F002, ..., CM-F001, ...) in both files, add the 5 new structured sections under each feature heading. Format:
  ```
  ### Inclusion
  - Default: optional
  - Mandatory when: [condition]
  - Conditional when: [condition]

  ### Success Criteria
  - [measurable outcome 1]
  - [measurable outcome 2]

  ### Failure Scenarios
  - [scenario: impact: mitigation]
  - [...]

  ### Cross-Tree Refs
  - See `_cross-tree-constraints.yaml` entries: [CTC-001, CTC-003]

  ### Experiential
  - Usage count: 0 (bootstrap)
  - Scenarios: (grows via /promote-to-kb)
  - Common mistakes: [...]
  ```
  Preserve existing prose (When It Matters, Depth Spectrum, Signals, Tradeoffs); the new sections go between Tradeoffs and the next feature.
- **Depends on:** T19
- **Expected Outcome:** Two of the five domain-taxonomy files carry the full structured extension.
- **Verification:** The linter task (T22) reports PASS for both files.

#### T21: Extend remaining `domain-taxonomy/*.md` files + write cross-tree constraints
- **Sub-issue:** 214.4
- **Files:**
  - Modify `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/memory/knowledge/domain-taxonomy/payments.md`

- **Details:** Apply the same extension as T20 to payments, personalization, and search markdown files. Write `_cross-tree-constraints.yaml` with â‰Ą5 bootstrap constraints in normalized form:
  ```yaml
  constraints:
    - id: CTC-001
      description: "High security profiles require MFA"
      condition: "project_profile.security_level in ['high', 'critical']"
      implies_include: ["UM-F004"]
      rationale: "NIST 800-63B"
    - id: CTC-002
      description: "Healthcare compliance requires audit logging"
      condition: "'HIPAA' in project_profile.compliance"
      implies_include: ["UM-F003"]
      rationale: "HHS 164.312 technical safeguards"
    ...
  ```
  Each constraint references a real feature ID from the extended markdown files. No dangling references.
- **Depends on:** T20
- **Expected Outcome:** All five domain-taxonomy files are extended; constraints file is bootstrapped.
- **Verification:** Linter PASS on all five files; constraint file yamllint clean; no dangling feature IDs.

The intent.yaml must carry: **metadata zone** â€” `name: spec-product`, `description: "Transform a product idea plus project profile into enriched intent epics via KB-driven capability configuration"`, `version: 0.1.0`, `checksum: <computed by compile step>`; **content zone** â€” `intent`, `constraints`, `failure_conditions`, `scenarios` only. Constraints should cover: product description length, profile completeness, KB accessibility (existence of extended domain-taxonomy files), delegation rules (all domain work via Market Analyst / Product Keeper / judge), whitelist compliance, kb_source traceability (every epic references a real feature ID like UM-F001), validator blocking, min â‰Ą2 success_scenarios and â‰Ą2 failure_scenarios per epic, quantified constraints, checkpoint artifacts, cross-tree constraint application, scriber dispatch for evidence, three-layer hierarchy, no agent-count budget (plays are just plays). Must absorb deprecated-play-patterns.md key patterns: pre-lock resolution gate, three-axis profile derivation, decision_points, deferred scope enforcement.

- **Expected Outcome:** Canonical intent.yaml with metadata + 4 content fields.
- **Verification:** grep for top-level keys finds EXACTLY `name`, `description`, `version`, `checksum`, `intent`, `constraints`, `failure_conditions`, `scenarios` (eight top-level keys) and nothing else; yamllint passes.

Constraints: NEVER invent capabilities outside the KB; NEVER skip a cross-tree constraint.

- **Details:** Create `validate-architecture-spec` skill: asserts every intent epic is traced to at least one architectural decision and every quality attribute in the quality profile has at least one addressing mechanism in the arch output. Run `/create-play --build build-arch` â€” compiler reads T37 intent, skill inventory (reuses existing tech-designer / tech-architect + the new validator), audits agents, selects workflow, produces compiled artifact + evals. `/create-play --review build-arch` â†’ G1-G11 PASS. End-to-end integration test: `/spec-product` â†’ `/design-product` â†’ `/build-arch` against a fixture. Confirm `architecture.yaml` and `quality-standards.yaml` produced under `.meridian/product/arch/` with explicit traces back to spec-product quality profile and design-product screen inventory. Grep `docs/` and `core/components/` for any remaining `prepare-architecture` references and remove them.
- **Depends on:** T37
- **Expected Outcome:** build-arch compiles cleanly; full three-play chain works end-to-end; no stale prepare-architecture references.
- **Verification:** G1-G11 PASS; integration produces arch artifacts with traces; grep for `prepare-architecture` returns zero matches in `docs/` and `core/components/`.
- **Rollback plan:** Delete build-arch directory; git restore prepare-architecture directory; git checkout on docs.

---

## Summary for Implementation

- **Scriber ships first** (214.1) so every subsequent play adopts the background-evidence pattern from day one.
- **Folder whitelist is strict** (214.2) â€” ADR 017 rejects the Plan sub-agent's amendment proposal. Ops files live inside `product/`, `ux/`, `arch/` buckets.
- **Config moves** (214.2) â€” `core/config.yaml` â†’ `.meridian/core/config.yaml`, with every reference updated across plays, agents, skills, docs, and memory workflows.
- **Play levels and agent budgets retired** (214.2) â€” CLAUDE.md section 4 rewritten: plays are just plays; no L1/L2, no â‰¤5-agent ceiling.
- **"Bake" terminology retired** (214.2) â€” `/create-play --bake` renamed (proposed: `--build`; final verb confirmed at approval gate). Every doc/skill reference updated.
- **Capture-then-delete** (214.3) protects accumulated production detail (resolution gates, three-axis profile, decision_points, quality-standards derivation from prepare-architecture) before the deprecated plays disappear.
- **KB extension, not parallel tree** (214.4) â€” existing `domain-taxonomy/*.md` files extended with 5 new sections; the ONLY new KB file is `_cross-tree-constraints.yaml`.
- **Plan-product and design-product are fresh builds** (214.5, 214.6) absorbing captured patterns, using the scriber pattern, and reading from the extended markdown KB.
- **Spec-arch is a fresh build replacing prepare-architecture** (214.7) â€” renamed for naming consistency with spec-product / design-product. Full rewrite, not a contract update.
- **Structural depth enforcement** via validators is non-negotiable; shallow outputs become structurally impossible.
- **Intent schema with metadata** â€” every new intent.yaml has name, description, version, checksum + 4 content fields.

## Critical Files for Implementation

- `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/.meridian/core/config.yaml` â€” moved location; whitelist reconciliation; blocks everything downstream (214.2).
- `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/agents/scriber.md` â€” utility agent every new play depends on (214.1).
- `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/memory/knowledge/deprecated-play-patterns.md` â€” captured value from soon-to-be-deleted plays (discover-product, plan-roadmap, discover-product-opportunity, prepare-architecture); input to new plays' intent crafting (214.3 + referenced by 214.7 for build-arch).
- `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/memory/standards/intent-yaml-schema.yaml` â€” canonical intent.yaml shape with metadata + 4 content fields (214.4).
- `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/memory/standards/kb-extension-conventions.md` â€” the 5 new markdown section headings and their structures (214.4).
- `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/memory/knowledge/domain-taxonomy/_cross-tree-constraints.yaml` â€” the ONLY new KB file; deterministic profile-driven configuration (214.4).
- `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/plays/spec-product/reference/intent.yaml` â€” metadata + 4 content fields; defines all constraints, failure conditions, scenarios (214.5).
- `/Users/kapilahuja/cto/builder/meridian-os/core/components/plays/design-product/reference/intent.yaml` â€” metadata + 4 content fields (214.6).
